posts.text:
There is a solution to stabilizing the debt: high-skilled immigration. 

It feels "nice" to use a family-sponsorship immigration model. But we must deal with the fiscal problems here at home. A talent-based immigration model will bring in ONLY those who contribute to our economy.

@DanielDiMartino did the math, which @RepDavid shared on the House floor today. Worth the watch:

Video

Why is San Francisco spending taxpayer money on all-black drag shows and massage therapy for black criminals? Why is Gavin Newsom building a $114 million "butterfly bridge" and why is it so far over budget?

MI senior fellow @christopherrufo discusses this & more on @FoxBusiness

Video

posts.text: New York City spends roughly $8 billion annually on nonprofit contracts—more than the entire NYPD budget—but sees limited accountability. A new MI issue brief by @joshjappel calls for a performance-based model tied to results.

posts.text:
ICYMI: MI's @tal_fortgang on today’s “civil terrorists” on the @CityJournal Podcast:

“It’s a broad way of describing a common thread: these are people who cast Americans and the U.S. government as villains in whatever they do.”

posts.text:
What forces are driving the ideological shift within America’s elite universities?

Join us April 13 for a conversation with @EliseStefanik on her new book Poisoned Ivies—and the stakes for academic freedom, intellectual diversity, and our civic culture.

posts.text:
MI's @PolicyEngineer on New York's current fiscal situation:

"More than half of the state budget goes to just two areas—Medicaid and school aid. Those are both areas where the state, historically, has done a terrible job making sure that it gets a good return on its investments."

Video
